title: Timing advance of wireless device
abstract: A wireless device starts, after receiving an indication to determine a timing advance (TA) of a second cell based on measuring a reception timing of one or more reference signals of the second cell, a time duration. The wireless device determines a failure of determination of the TA of the second cell based on an expiration of the time duration without a successful determination of the TA.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method comprising: receiving, by a wireless device, a radio resource control (RRC) message comprising: configuration parameters of a second cell; and a parameter indicating a time duration for determining (acquiring) a timing advance (TA) of the second cell; starting the time duration after receiving an indication to determine the TA of the second cell; during the time duration, monitoring at least one of: at least one first reference signal of a first cell; or at least one second reference signal of the second cell; and determining an expiration of the time duration without a successful determination of the TA of the second cell; and after the expiration of the time duration, transmitting at least one of: an indication of a failure of determining the TA of the second cell; or a preamble for a random access to the second cell. 2. A method comprising: starting, by a wireless device and after receiving an indication to determine a timing advance (TA) of a second cell based on measuring a reception timing of one or more reference signals of the second cell, a time duration; and determining a failure of determination of the TA of the second cell based on an expiration of the time duration without a successful determination of the TA. 3. The method of claim 2, further comprising receiving, by the wireless device the indication to determine the TA of the second cell. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the indication is received in a radio resource control (RRC) message comprising configuration parameters of the second cell. 5. The method of one of claims 2 to 4, further comprising monitoring, during the time duration, at least one of: at least one first reference signal of a first cell; or 

 at least one second reference signal of the second cell. 6. The method of one of claims 2 to 5, further comprising transmitting, after the expiration of the time duration, at least one of: an indication of the failure of determining the TA of the second cell; or a preamble for a random access to the second cell. 7. The method of one of claims 2 to 6, further comprising transmitting, by the wireless device to a first base station, an uplink message comprising a parameter indicating the time duration for determining the TA of the second cell. 8. The method of one of claims 2 to 7, wherein a first base station and/or a first distributed unit of the first base station transmits, to the wireless device and based on receiving an indication of the failure of determining the TA of the second cell, at least one of. an indication to perform the cell switch to the second cell based on a random access that comprises at least one of: transmitting by the wireless device a preamble via the second cell; or receiving by the wireless device a random access response comprising a field indicating a TA value for the second cell; or an indication to perform an early TA acquisition procedure that comprises at least one of: transmitting by the wireless device a preamble via the second cell; or receiving by the wireless device a field indicating a TA value for the second cell 9. The method of one of claims 2 to 8, further comprising transmitting, after the expiration of the time duration, a preamble via the second cell for at least one of: a random access, to the second cell, comprising at least one of: transmitting by the wireless device the preamble via the second cell; or receiving by the wireless device a random access response comprising a field indicating a TA value for the second cell; or an early TA acquisition procedure that comprises at least one of: transmitting by the wireless device the preamble via the second cell; or receiving by the wireless device a field indicating a TA value for the second cell. 10. The method of one of claims 2 to 9, wherein the indication is received in a radio resource control (RRC) message comprising at least one parameter indicating to perform a cell switch from the first cell to the second cell based on: a determined TA of the second cell; and the configuration parameters of the second cell. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the RRC message comprises at least one of: a parameter indicating that the cell switch is based on a layer 1 and/or layer 2 triggered mobility (LTM) procedure; parameters for the LTM procedure; or parameters for determining the TA of the second cell based on reference signals of the first cell and/or the second cell. 12. The method of one of claims 10 to 11, wherein the performing the cell switch comprises transmitting an RRC reconfiguration complete message via the second cell based on the determined TA of the second cell. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the transmitting the RRC reconfiguration complete message comprises transmitting the RRC reconfiguration complete message via resources of the second cell, the resources comprising at least one of: at least one resource indicated by a dynamic grant; or at least one periodic resource. 14. The method of one of claims 2 to 13, wherein the TA of the second cell comprises an uplink TA of the wireless device for the second cell. 15. The method of one of claims 2 to 14, wherein the determining the TA of the second cell comprises determining a TA of a cell group (e.g., TAG, etc.) comprising the second cell 16. The method of one of claims 2 to 15, wherein the determining the TA of the second cell is based on measuring a reception timing of at least one of: a parameter indicating that the cell switch is based on a layer 1 and/or layer 2 triggered mobility (LTM) procedure; parameters for the LTM procedure; or 

 parameters for determining the TA of the second cell based on reference signals of the first cell and/or the second cell. 17. The method of one of claims 2 to 16, wherein the determining a failure of determining the TA of the second cell comprises not determining the TA of the second cell during an entirety of the time duration. 18. The method of one of claims 2 to 16, wherein the determining a failure of determining the TA of the second cell comprises, for one or more occasions, of one or more reference signals from the second cell and during the time duration, comprising: the wireless device fails determining a reception timing of a corresponding reference signal; or a received power of a corresponding reference signal is equal to or lower than a first power value. 19. A wireless device comprising one or more processors and memory storing instructions that, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the wireless device to perform the method of any of claims 1 to 18. 20. A non-transitory computer-readable medium comprising instructions that, when executed by one or more processors, cause a wireless device to perform the method of any of claims 1 to 18. 21. A method comprising: receiving, by a wireless device, a radio resource control (RRC) message comprising: configuration parameters of a second cell; and a field indicating a time duration for determining a timing advance (TA) of the second cell; starting the time duration after receiving an indication to determine the TA of the second cell; during the time duration, determining the TA of the second cell based on at least one of: at least one first reference signal of a first cell; or at least one second reference signal of the second cell; and performing a cell switch from the first cell to the second cell based on: the determined TA of the second cell; and the configuration parameters of the second cell. 22. The method of claim 21 , wherein the performing the cell switch comprises transmitting an RRC reconfiguration complete message via the second cell based on the determined TA of the second cell. 23. The method of one of claims 21 to 22, wherein the determining the TA of the second cell is based on measuring a reception timing of at least one of: the at least one first reference signal of a first cell; the at least one second reference signal of the second cell; 

 at least one reference signal of at least one cell of a first cell group comprising the first cell; or at least one reference signal of at least one cell of a second cell group comprising the second cell. 24. The method of one of claims 21 to 23, further comprising stopping a timer associated with the time duration in response to successfully determining the TA of the second cell. 25. The method of one of claims 21 to 24, further comprising receiving a medium access control control element (MAC CE) indicating the cell switch, wherein the performing the cell switch is in response to receiving the MAC CE. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the MAC CE comprises the indication to determine the TA of the second cell. 27. The method of one of claims 21 to 26, wherein the receiving the indication to determine the TA of the second cell comprises receiving a downlink control information, via a physical downlink control channel (PDCCH), comprising the indication to determine the TA of the second cell. 28. The method of one of claims 21 to 27, wherein the RRC message comprises at least one of: a field indicating that the cell switch is based on a layer 1 and/or layer 2 triggered mobility (LTM) procedure; parameters for the LTM procedure; or parameters for determining the TA of the second cell based on reference signals of the first cell and/or the second cell. 29. The method of one of claims 21 to 28, wherein the RRC message comprises parameters indicating configured grant resources of the second cell, the transmitting the RRC reconfiguration complete message being via the configured grant resources of the second cell. 30. A wireless device comprising one or more processors and memory storing instructions that, when executed by the one or more processors, cause the wireless device to perform the method of any of claims 21 to 29. 31. A non-transitory computer-readable medium comprising instructions that, when executed by one or more processors, cause a wireless device to perform the method of any of claims 21 to 29. 32. A method comprising: receiving, by a wireless device, a radio resource control (RRC) message comprising: configuration parameters of a second cell; and 

 a field indicating a first number associated with determining a timing advance (TA) of the second cell; after receiving an indication to determine the TA of the second cell, monitoring one or more occasions of one or more reference signals, wherein the one or more reference signals comprises at least one of: at least one first reference signal of a first cell; or at least one second reference signal of the second cell; and for each of the one or more occasions, determining a reference signal reception failure based on whether a received power of the reference signal is less than or equal to a first power value; and determining, based on a number of reference signal reception failures reaching the first number, a failure of determining the TA of the second cell. 33. A method comprising: receiving, by a wireless device, a message comprising a parameter indicating a first number for determining a timing advance (TA) of a second cell based on measuring a reception timing of one or more reference signals of the second cell; determining a reference signal reception failure based on whether a received power of a reference signal of the second cell is less than or equal to a first power value; and based on a number of reference signal reception failures reaching the first number, determining a failure of determining the TA of the second cell. 34. A method comprising: determining a failed occasion based on whether a received power of a reference signal of a second cell is less than or equal to a first power value; and based on a number of failed occasions reaching a first number, determining a failure of determining a timing advance (TA) of the second cell. 35. The method of claim 34, further comprising receiving, by the wireless device, a message comprising a parameter indicating the first number for determining the TA of the second cell based on measuring a reception timing of one or more reference signals of the second cell. 36. The method of claim 35, wherein the determining the failed occasion is based on a failure of the determining the reception timing. 37. The method of one of claims 35 to 36, wherein the message comprises a parameter indicating the first power value. 38. The method of one of claims 34 to 37, further comprising transmitting, by the wireless device to a first base station, an uplink message comprising at least one of. a parameter indicating the first number for determining the TA of the second cell based on measuring a reception timing of one or more reference signals of the second cell; or the first power value.
